query_normalized: the impact of social comparison on self-perception in ai-generated image platforms psychology
rationale: The paper focuses on the technical application of multitask learning algorithms to detect stress and depression from social media text, which is an off-domain computational task unrelated to the psychological mechanism of "social comparison" or its specific impact on "self-perception" in the context of AI-generated images. It fails to satisfy any inclusion criteria as it does not measure the relevant psychological variables, establish a baseline for the specific phenomenon, or provide foundation
